sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund 12 months of Eternl operations, maintenance, and improvements, including frontend and backend work, infrastructure, user support, administration, and audits/oversight.

Deliverables: Frontend maintenance and cross-platform development for Chrome extension, web/PWA, iOS, Android, beta channel, CIPs, hardfork features, multisig, dApp interoperability, app store compliance, and hardware wallet support, Backend infrastructure and operations including multi-region servers, Cardano nodes, DBSync indexers, application servers, metadata aggregation, Hub backend services, monitoring, alerting, and incident response, Governance tooling enhancements including DRep dashboards, proposal browsers, in-wallet voting, and proposal creation tooling, User support through Discord ticketing, Telegram, public channels, ecosystem project support, wiki maintenance, and educational videos, Day-to-day operations including release management, hardware wallet vendor coordination, dApp/CIP coordination, code review, and security practices, Periodic independent audits of treasury fund use and implementation of oversight metrics
Deadline/expiry: 12-month delivery period, August 2026 to July 2027

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS used for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover approximately $420,000 in annual costs for 12 months.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The requested budget is allocated as 55% frontend, 25% backend, 5% support, 13% admin, and 2% audits.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal funds 6.0 FTE across 10 contributors at $70,000 per FTE.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl plans to introduce Pro plans priced at $96/year for personal users and $360/year for company users, and asserts that roughly 5,500 users, about 4.2% of its install base, would fully cover the $420,000 annual cost.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl states it has about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 Android/iOS installs.
missing: Independent evidence for: Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS used for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction.
Independent evidence for: Eternl states it has about 100,000 browser-extension installs and about 30,000 Android/iOS installs.

Independent evidence for: Eternl asserts that 10-18% of Cardano mainnet transactions are conducted via Eternl and that Eternl has provided multi-platform access with basically zero downtime for over five years.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover approximately $420,000 in annual costs for 12 months." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Eternl is a non-custodial Cardano light wallet for web, browser extension, Android, and iOS used for payments, staking, governance, and DApp interaction." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
